channel.title: Kostas Stathoulopoulos
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License.

item.title: Prototype app: ACL conference explorer
item.description: I made this app ahead of the ACL 2020 conference. It is intended for the visual exploration and discovery of research publications that have been presented at it. Every particle in the scatterplot is an academic publication. The particles are positioned in space based on the semantic similarity of the paper titles; the closer two points are, the more semantically similar their titles. You can hover over the particles to read their titles and you can click them to be redirected to the original source.

item.title: Orion: Web interface demo
item.description: Zac Ioannidis, my collaborator on Orion, recorded a walkthrough of Orion’s frontend for the IC2S2 conference where we are running a tutorial. Watch the video here.

item.title: IC2S2 | Tutorial on transformers, UMAP, Faiss and HDBSCAN
item.description: Tutorial for the IC2S2 on how to encode text data with transformers and use the embeddings in downstream tasks such as clustering with HDBSCAN, dimensionality reduction with UMAP and vector similarity with Faiss. Link to video

item.title: About
item.description: Hi, I am Kostas! I am a Mozilla Open Science Fellow and a Principal Researcher (Data Science) at Nesta, a UK-based non-profit research institute. I work at the intersection of information retrieval, data engineering and scientometrics. I enjoy developing tools that help policymakers take evidence-based decisions and users navigate information systems. I am currently working on Orion, a knowledge discovery and research measurement tool. I am spending most of my time on developing:

item.title: IC2S2 | A large-scale analysis of the misinformation research landscape
item.description: Presentation at the IC2S2. I examined the geography and composition of misinformation research using publications from Microsoft Academic Graph. I collected the data using Orion, a research measurement and knowledge discovery tool I’ve been developing for Mozilla. Link to video

item.title: Prototype app: Misinformation research explorer
item.description: This application is intended for visual exploration and discovery of research publications on misinformation, disinformation and fake news. Every particle in the visualisation is an academic publication. The particles are positioned in space based on the semantic similarity of the paper abstracts; the closer two points are, the more semantically similar their abstracts. You can hover over the particles to read their titles and you can click them to be redirected to the original source.

item.title: Is AI causing collective intelligence research to become less diverse?
item.description: AI is increasingly being used within collective intelligence research and practice. Our analysis of almost 40,000 research articles published in the last 20 years reveals the trends in this fast growing field. Research at the crossover between the fields shows little topic diversity or disciplinary breadth and this may be having a spillover effect on non-AI collective intelligence research. We also find that industry and, increasingly, China are setting the trajectory.

item.title: SciNLP | Orion: An interactive information retrieval system for scientific knowledge discovery
item.description: Short presentation on Orion, an interactive information retrieval system for scientific texts that I am developing for Mozilla. Link to video
